title: Market engagement: Legal Aid for Inquests
body: "The government has announced plans for the largest expansion of legal aid in a decade, ensuring bereaved families have access to non-means-tested support during inquests.  The LAA is inviting views from organisations currently providing or interested in providing advice and representation under the Public Office (Accountability) Bill proposals.  Why is it happening now?  The LAA wants to ensure that services are in place as soon as are required once the Bill becomes law.  Market engagement will gather feedback to inform future service design and assess providers’ capacity, capability and interest in delivering work under the proposed legislation.  Where can I find out more?  The LAA will be running the following sessions:   Thursday 13 November, 4:30pm to 5:30pm Monday 17 November, 5:00pm to 6:00pm   To express your interest in joining one of these sessions please email us at civil.contracts@justice.gov.uk to confirm:   the name of your organisation the session you would like to attend the name and email address of each attendee   An event link will be shared with you once your place has been confirmed.  Unable to attend?  If you are unable to attend in person, we would still like to hear from you.  Please contact us at civil.contracts@justice.gov.uk  Further information  For further information regarding the Public Office (Accountability) Bill, please visit Hillsborough Law to ensure truth never concealed by state again - GOV.UK  "
